objective: Create internal/provider/manifest.go (NEW package `provider`) defining the canonical `Manifest` struct carrying ALL PRD §12.1 schema fields (Name, Detect, Command, Subcommand, PromptDelivery stdin|positional|flag, PromptFlag, PrintFlag, ModelFlag, DefaultModel, SystemPromptFlag, ProviderFlag, DefaultProvider, BareFlags, Output raw|json, JSONField, StripCodeFence, RetryInstruction, Env map[string]string) with `toml:"..."` tags matching the §12.1 snake_case keys, plus a `Rendered{Args []string, StdinPayload string, DeliverViaStdin bool, Env []string}` result type and a PURE method `func (m Manifest) Render(model, provider, sys, user string) (*Rendered, error)` implementing the §12.2 command-rendering algorithm BYTE-EXACTLY (the order in PRD §12.2 + decisions.md §5: Subcommand…, (ProviderFlag,provider), (ModelFlag,model), (SystemPromptFlag,sys), BareFlags…, PrintFlag; then a delivery switch). System-prompt-via-payload fallback: when SystemPromptFlag=="" && sys!="" the effective payload becomes sys+"\n\n"+user for stdin/positional/flag alike. Render does NOT resolve defaults and does NOT import os/exec (NO sh -c is an executor concern, §19); Rendered.Args EXCLUDES the command (executor does exec.Command(m.Command, r.Args...)) and Rendered.Env is the manifest env additions as sorted "K=V" strings. Deliver golden-table tests (internal/provider/manifest_test.go, package provider, stdlib testing) that use the external_deps.md §B.1-B.6 Rendered lines as oracle for the flag SET and assert the algorithm-derived arg ORDER + StdinPayload byte-exact for pi/claude/gemini/opencode/codex/cursor, covering stdin-vs-positional, with/without system_prompt_flag (prepend fallback), with/without provider+model, plus flag-delivery, Env-map sorting, unknown-delivery error, and empty-sys no-prepend. DOCS impact: Mode A — godoc on the Manifest schema fields citing PRD §12.1/§12.2.
context:
# PRP — P1.M2.T1.S1: internal/provider/manifest.go — Manifest struct + Render

## Goal

**Feature Goal**: Define the canonical provider-manifest type for stagehand — a `Manifest` struct (PRD §12.1 schema, all fields) plus a `Rendered` result type and a pure `Manifest.Render(model, provider, sys, user string) (*Rendered, error)` method that turns a logical intent ("run agent X with this model/provider/system-prompt/user-payload, bare and ephemeral") into an exact arg slice + stdin payload + env additions, per the §12.2 command-rendering algorithm, byte-for-byte. This is the foundational type the entire provider subsystem (built-ins, registry, executor) and the config override-merge (M5) are built on.

**Deliverable**: Two new files in a NEW `internal/provider` package:
- `internal/provider/manifest.go` — `package provider` doc comment + the `Manifest` struct (all §12.1 fields, `toml:"..."` tags) + the `Rendered` struct + delivery/output string constants + `func (m Manifest) Render(model, provider, sys, user string) (*Rendered, error)`.
- `internal/provider/manifest_test.go` — `package provider` golden-table tests (stdlib `testing` only) with all six providers + edge cases.

**Success Definition**: `go test ./internal/provider/` passes; the golden table asserts byte-exact `Args` (algorithm-derived order) and `StdinPayload` for pi/claude/gemini/opencode/codex/cursor derived from external_deps.md §B.1-B.6; edge cases (no model, no provider, flag delivery, env sorting, unknown-delivery error, empty-sys no-prepend) pass. `go build ./internal/provider/`, `go vet ./internal/provider/` clean; `gofmt -l internal/provider/` empty. stdlib-only imports (`fmt`, `sort`, `strings` are the only likely needs — NO `os/exec`, NO cobra/pflag/go-toml). No go.mod/go.sum change; main.go/Makefile/ui untouched; no import cycle (config M5 will import this, not vice-versa).

## Why

- **The provider system's keystone type.** Every other piece of `internal/provider` consumes `Manifest`/`Rendered`: the six built-in manifests (M2.T3.S1), the registry override-merge (M2.T3.S2), the executor that turns `Rendered` into an `exec.Cmd` (M2.T4.S1), the output parser that reads `Output`/`JSONField`/`StripCodeFence` (M2.T2.S1), and the config override-merge that imports this canonical type (M5.T3.S1). Getting the schema + render algorithm byte-exact here unblocks all of them.
- **§12.2 algorithm is the contract.** The PRD pins the exact arg ordering (subcommand → provider → model → system_prompt → bare_flags → print_flag) and the delivery switch. decisions.md §5 restates it identically. `commit-pi` is byte-identical to the algorithm's pi output (external_deps.md §B.1) — proof the algorithm is the source of truth for ORDER.
- **Security is structural here.** By rendering to a `[]string` (Args) and a stdin payload (never interpolating the diff into an argument), this type is what makes PRD §19's "No shell interpolation / no sh -c" hold downstream. Render must produce a clean arg slice, never a shell string.
- **Pure + testable.** Render takes already-resolved strings and returns data; it does no I/O and imports no `os/exec`. That makes the byte-exact golden table possible without spawning any process. The os/exec concerns (exec.Command, Setpgid, timeout, os.Environ merge) are the executor's (M2.T4.S1).

## What (technical requirements)

1. NEW file `internal/provider/manifest.go`, NEW `package provider`. It MUST carry the `// Package provider ...` doc comment (it is the first/primary file of the package; sibling files builtin.go/registry.go/executor.go/parse.go added by later tasks use a plain `package provider` line, mirroring how internal/ui/exitcode.go owns its package doc).
2. **Manifest struct** — ALL §12.1 fields, exported, idiomatic CamelCase Go names, each with a `toml:"<snake_key>"` tag matching the §12.1 TOML schema and a godoc comment citing §12.1:
   `Name`, `Detect`, `Command` (string); `Subcommand`, `BareFlags` ([]string); `PromptDelivery` (string, values "stdin"|"positional"|"flag"); `PromptFlag`, `PrintFlag`, `ModelFlag`, `DefaultModel`, `SystemPromptFlag`, `ProviderFlag`, `DefaultProvider` (string); `Output` (string, values "raw"|"json"); `JSONField`, `RetryInstruction` (string); `StripCodeFence` (bool); `Env` (map[string]string, `toml:"env"`).
3. **Exported string constants** for the enumerated field values, for readability + testability: `DeliveryStdin="stdin"`, `DeliveryPositional="positional"`, `DeliveryFlag="flag"`, `OutputRaw="raw"`, `OutputJSON="json"`.
4. **Rendered struct** — exactly `{ Args []string; StdinPayload string; DeliverViaStdin bool; Env []string }` with godoc. `Args` is the arg slice AFTER the command (the executor does `exec.Command(m.Command, r.Args...)`). `Env` is the manifest's env additions formatted as sorted `"KEY=VALUE"` strings (the executor appends to `os.Environ()`).
5. **Render method** `func (m Manifest) Render(model, provider, sys, user string) (*Rendered, error)` implementing §12.2 byte-exactly. Pseudocode (see Implementation Blueprint for the full listing):
   - `effUser := user`; `if m.SystemPromptFlag == "" && sys != "" { effUser = sys + "\n\n" + user }` (system-prompt-via-payload fallback — applies to ALL delivery modes, per reference_impl.md §3 and the §B positional examples).
   - `args := append([]string(nil), m.Subcommand...)` (defensive copy so BareFlags/append never mutate the caller's slice).
   - `if m.ProviderFlag != "" && provider != "" { args = append(args, m.ProviderFlag, provider) }`
   - `if m.ModelFlag != "" && model != "" { args = append(args, m.ModelFlag, model) }`
   - `if m.SystemPromptFlag != "" && sys != "" { args = append(args, m.SystemPromptFlag, sys) }`
   - `args = append(args, m.BareFlags...)` (VERBATIM, including empty-string values like claude's `--tools ""`).
   - `if m.PrintFlag != "" { args = append(args, m.PrintFlag) }`
   - delivery switch on `m.PromptDelivery`:
     - `""` or `DeliveryStdin` → `StdinPayload = effUser`, `DeliverViaStdin = true`, nothing appended (treat `""` as stdin = the §12.1 default).
     - `DeliveryPositional` → `args = append(args, effUser)`, `DeliverViaStdin = false`, `StdinPayload = ""`.
     - `DeliveryFlag` → `args = append(args, m.PromptFlag, effUser)`, `DeliverViaStdin = false`, `StdinPayload = ""`.
     - default → `return nil, fmt.Errorf("provider %q: unknown prompt_delivery %q (want stdin|positional|flag)", m.Name, m.PromptDelivery)`.
   - `env := make([]string, 0, len(m.Env))`; for each key in SORTED order append `key+"="+m.Env[key]`; set `r.Env = env` (sorted ⇒ deterministic).
   - `return &Rendered{Args: args, StdinPayload: ..., DeliverViaStdin: ..., Env: env}, nil`.
6. Render must NOT: import `os/exec`; resolve `DefaultModel`/`DefaultProvider` (caller's job); validate `Command` (registry/executor's job); read `Output`/`JSONField`/`StripCodeFence`/`RetryInstruction` (consumed by parseOutput M2.T2.S1 and the retry loop); build an `exec.Cmd` (executor's job). It IS a pure renderer.
7. NEW file `internal/provider/manifest_test.go`, `package provider`, stdlib `testing` ONLY. Table-driven golden tests + targeted edge tests (see Implementation Blueprint for the full case list and exact expected values).

### Success Criteria
- [ ] `internal/provider/manifest.go` exists as the first file of a NEW `package provider` with a `// Package provider` doc comment citing PRD §12; imports only stdlib (no os/exec, no cobra/pflag/go-toml).
- [ ] `Manifest` struct has ALL §12.1 fields with exact names + `toml:"<snake_key>"` tags + godoc citing §12.1; `Rendered{Args, StdinPayload, DeliverViaStdin, Env}` exists; delivery/output string constants exist.
- [ ] `Render(model, provider, sys, user string) (*Rendered, error)` implements the §12.2 order (subcommand, provider, model, system_prompt, bare_flags, print_flag) + delivery switch byte-exactly; system-prompt prepend fallback fires when `SystemPromptFlag=="" && sys!=""` for all delivery modes; unknown delivery returns an error.
- [ ] Golden-table tests assert byte-exact `Args` (algorithm-derived order) + `StdinPayload` + `DeliverViaStdin` for pi/claude/gemini/opencode/codex/cursor from external_deps.md §B.1-B.6.
- [ ] Edge tests cover: no-model (skip --model), no-provider (skip --provider), flag delivery, Env-map sorted "K=V", unknown-delivery error, stdin-no-sys-flag + empty sys (no stray "\n\n").
- [ ] `go test ./internal/provider/` and `go test ./...` exit 0; `go build ./internal/provider/` and `go vet ./internal/provider/` clean; `gofmt -l internal/provider/` empty.
- [ ] Mode A docs satisfied: godoc on Manifest schema fields cites PRD §12.1/§12.2. No README/docs created; main.go/Makefile/go.mod/go.sum/internal/ui untouched; no `go mod tidy`.

## All Needed Context

### Known gotchas
```go
// CRITICAL (ORDER RECONCILIATION): the §B.2 (claude) and §B.6 (cursor) illustrative
// "Rendered:" lines place print_flag FIRST (verbatim from the proven shell scripts).
// The §12.2 algorithm + decisions.md §5 + this work item ALL place print_flag AFTER
// bare_flags. The work item mandates "§12.2 byte-exactly", so print_flag goes LAST.
// pi (§B.1) matches the algorithm literally and is the canonical proof. For claude/
// cursor, assert the ALGORITHM-derived order in the golden tests; the §B lines are the
// oracle only for the flag SET, not their literal ordering. Flag order is
// parse-order-independent for those CLIs, so this is functionally identical.

// CRITICAL (PREPEND APPLIES TO POSITIONAL TOO): the work-item text names the
// sys+"\n\n"+user prepend under the stdin branch, but reference_impl.md §3 + the §B
// gemini/opencode/cursor positional examples prove the prepend also applies to
// positional and flag delivery. Implement ONE effective-payload computation and use it
// for all three delivery modes. Guard with `&& sys != ""` to avoid a stray "\n\n".

// CRITICAL (Rendered.Args EXCLUDES the command): Rendered has no Command field. The
// executor (M2.T4.S1) holds the Manifest alongside and calls exec.Command(m.Command,
// r.Args...). Golden tests must assert Args WITHOUT the command token.

// CRITICAL (Render does NOT resolve defaults): the §12.2 algorithm only guards
// `model != ""`/`provider != ""`; it never references DefaultModel/DefaultProvider.
// Render receives already-resolved values. Default resolution is the executor's job.

// GOTCHA (Env determinism): Manifest.Env is map[string]string (random iteration).
// Rendered.Env MUST be sorted by key so output is byte-stable + testable. Format as
// "KEY=VALUE". The executor merges with os.Environ(); Render must NOT call os.Environ
// (that would make it impure + untestable + an os import).

// GOTCHA (verbatim bare_flags): claude's BareFlags contain EMPTY strings
// ("--setting-sources","","--tools","",...). Append BareFlags verbatim — empty-string
// elements are significant (claude interprets `--tools ""` as disable-all-tools). Do
// NOT filter empties. The `if PrintFlag != ""` guard is about the FLAG NAME, not
// bare_flag values.

// GOTCHA (defensive copy): start args from `append([]string(nil), m.Subcommand...)`
// so a later `append(args, m.BareFlags...)` cannot mutate the caller's Subcommand slice.
// Do not alias manifest slices into the returned Rendered.

// GOTCHA (package doc): manifest.go OWNS the `// Package provider` doc comment.
// Do NOT repeat it in manifest_test.go or any later-added file (duplicate-package-
// comment lint), exactly like internal/ui/exitcode.go owns its package doc.

// GOTCHA (no go mod tidy): this file is stdlib-only; tidy is irrelevant and (per
// P1.M1.T1.S1) risky — it can strip go-toml which has no importer until M5.
```

## Implementation Blueprint

### Data models / structure
```go
// internal/provider/manifest.go
package provider // doc comment on the file

// Delivery modes (Manifest.PromptDelivery) and output formats (Manifest.Output).
const (
    DeliveryStdin      = "stdin"
    DeliveryPositional = "positional"
    DeliveryFlag       = "flag"
    OutputRaw          = "raw"
    OutputJSON         = "json"
)

// Manifest (PRD §12.1) — the canonical provider description config (M5) imports.
type Manifest struct {
    Name             string            `toml:"name"`
    Detect           string            `toml:"detect"`
    Command          string            `toml:"command"`
    Subcommand       []string          `toml:"subcommand"`
    PromptDelivery   string            `toml:"prompt_delivery"`
    PromptFlag       string            `toml:"prompt_flag"`
    PrintFlag        string            `toml:"print_flag"`
    ModelFlag        string            `toml:"model_flag"`
    DefaultModel     string            `toml:"default_model"`
    SystemPromptFlag string            `toml:"system_prompt_flag"`
    ProviderFlag     string            `toml:"provider_flag"`
    DefaultProvider  string            `toml:"default_provider"`
    BareFlags        []string          `toml:"bare_flags"`
    Output           string            `toml:"output"`
    JSONField        string            `toml:"json_field"`
    StripCodeFence   bool              `toml:"strip_code_fence"`
    RetryInstruction string            `toml:"retry_instruction"`
    Env              map[string]string `toml:"env"`
}

// Rendered is the pure result of Manifest.Render — consumed by the executor (M2.T4.S1).
type Rendered struct {
    Args            []string // arg slice AFTER the command
    StdinPayload    string   // fed to stdin when DeliverViaStdin
    DeliverViaStdin bool
    Env             []string // manifest env additions, sorted "KEY=VALUE"
}
```

### Implementation Patterns
```go
// internal/provider/manifest.go — the core Render body (PRD §12.2; decisions.md §5).
func (m Manifest) Render(model, provider, sys, user string) (*Rendered, error) {
    // System-prompt-via-payload fallback (§12.2 note; reference_impl.md §3): when the
    // agent has NO system-prompt flag, prepend sys to the payload for ALL delivery modes.
    effUser := user
    if m.SystemPromptFlag == "" && sys != "" {
        effUser = sys + "\n\n" + user
    }

    // Order is fixed by §12.2: subcommand, provider, model, system_prompt, bare, print.
    args := make([]string, 0, len(m.Subcommand)+len(m.BareFlags)+10)
    args = append(args, m.Subcommand...)
    if m.ProviderFlag != "" && provider != "" {
        args = append(args, m.ProviderFlag, provider)
    }
    if m.ModelFlag != "" && model != "" {
        args = append(args, m.ModelFlag, model)
    }
    if m.SystemPromptFlag != "" && sys != "" {
        args = append(args, m.SystemPromptFlag, sys)
    }
    args = append(args, m.BareFlags...) // VERBATIM incl. empty strings (claude --tools "")
    if m.PrintFlag != "" {
        args = append(args, m.PrintFlag) // AFTER bare_flags (algorithm; not the §B.2/§B.6 illustration)
    }

    r := &Rendered{Args: args}

    switch m.PromptDelivery {
    case "", DeliveryStdin: // "" = the §12.1 default
        r.StdinPayload = effUser
        r.DeliverViaStdin = true
    case DeliveryPositional:
        r.Args = append(r.Args, effUser)
    case DeliveryFlag:
        r.Args = append(r.Args, m.PromptFlag, effUser)
    default:
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("provider %q: unknown prompt_delivery %q (want stdin|positional|flag)", m.Name, m.PromptDelivery)
    }

    // Deterministic env additions (sorted); executor appends to os.Environ().
    if len(m.Env) > 0 {
        keys := make([]string, 0, len(m.Env))
        for k := range m.Env {
            keys = append(keys, k)
        }
        sort.Strings(keys)
        r.Env = make([]string, 0, len(keys))
        for _, k := range keys {
            r.Env = append(r.Env, k+"="+m.Env[k])
        }
    }
    return r, nil
}
```

### Integration Points
```yaml
PACKAGE: github.com/dustin/stagehand/internal/provider   # this task CREATES the package
CANONICAL TYPE: provider.Manifest — imported by config (M5.T3.S1) for override-merge; no import cycle (provider imports nothing internal)
CONSUMERS (downstream, DO NOT implement here):
  - internal/provider/executor.go (M2.T4.S1): r := m.Render(model,provider,sys,payload); exec.Command(m.Command, r.Args...); cmd.Stdin = bytes.NewReader(r.StdinPayload) if r.DeliverViaStdin else nil; cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), r.Env...); SysProcAttr{Setpgid:true}; default model/provider resolution lives HERE (if model=="" { model=m.DefaultModel }) BEFORE Render.
  - internal/provider/builtin.go (M2.T3.S1): the six §B manifests as Manifest literals.
  - internal/provider/registry.go (M2.T3.S2): field-by-field override merge into Manifest.
  - internal/provider/parse.go (M2.T2.S1): reads m.Output/m.JSONField/m.StripCodeFence.
NOT HERE: os/exec, os.Environ(), exec.Cmd, SysProcAttr, timeout, default resolution, TOML parsing (M5).
DOCS: Mode A — godoc on Manifest fields cites PRD §12.1; Render cites §12.2. Create NO README/docs (the PROVIDERS.md surface is fed in M8.T4.S2 from builtin.go).
```

## Validation Loop

### Level 1 — Syntax/Style/Build
- `go build ./internal/provider/` → exits 0 (new package compiles).
- `go vet ./internal/provider/` → clean.
- `test -z "$(gofmt -l internal/provider/)"` → empty (gofmt -l always exits 0; wrap in test -z — proven idiom from P1.M1.T2.S1/S2).

### Level 2 — Unit Tests (the contract verification)
- `go test ./internal/provider/` → PASS (6-provider golden table + all edge cases).
- `go test ./...` → whole-module integrity (Makefile `test` target green; internal/ui tests still pass).

### Level 3 — (none; Render is a pure in-process function — no process/IO to integration-test. The os/exec integration belongs to the executor, M2.T4.S1.)

## DOCS Impact
**Mode A.** Per the work item: godoc on the Manifest schema fields cites PRD §12.1; the Render method doc cites §12.2 and the system-prompt-via-payload fallback. This is the only documentation this task produces (inline godoc). Do NOT create README.md, docs/, or providers/*.toml here (those surfaces are fed by builtin.go in M2.T3.S1 / M8.T1.S1 and synced in M8.T4.S2). This PRP surfaces the item's declared DOCS line so it is not silently dropped.

## Anti-patterns to avoid
- ❌ Do NOT order args to match the §B.2/§B.6 illustrative "Rendered:" lines (print_flag first) — the §12.2 algorithm (print_flag after bare_flags) governs; pi (§B.1) is the literal proof.
- ❌ Do NOT limit the sys-prepend fallback to stdin only — it applies to positional and flag delivery too (§B gemini/opencode/cursor).
- ❌ Do NOT include the command token in Rendered.Args, nor add a Command field to Rendered — the executor supplies m.Command.
- ❌ Do NOT resolve DefaultModel/DefaultProvider, validate Command, or build an exec.Cmd inside Render — those are executor/registry concerns.
- ❌ Do NOT import os/exec, os (for Environ), cobra, pflag, or go-toml — Render is stdlib-only and pure.
- ❌ Do NOT filter empty strings out of BareFlags (claude relies on `--tools ""`).
- ❌ Do NOT leave Env unsorted (map iteration is random → non-deterministic, untestable output).
- ❌ Do NOT alias the caller's Subcommand/BareFlags slices into the result (defensive-copy first).
- ❌ Do NOT add fields beyond the §12.1 list (e.g. no `experimental`), and do NOT omit any listed field.
- ❌ Do NOT repeat the `// Package provider` doc comment in manifest_test.go or later files; do NOT touch main.go/Makefile/go.mod/go.sum/internal/ui; do NOT run `go mod tidy`.
implementationSteps: Create `internal/provider/manifest.go` as the FIRST file of a NEW `package provider` (the directory does not exist yet — create it). Write a file-level `// Package provider ...` doc comment citing PRD §12 (manifest.go OWNS the package doc; later-added files builtin.go/registry.go/executor.go/parse.go use a plain `package provider` line). Import ONLY stdlib (`fmt`, `sort`, `strings` as needed — NO `os/exec`, NO cobra/pflag/go-toml). Define exported string constants `DeliveryStdin="stdin"`, `DeliveryPositional="positional"`, `DeliveryFlag="flag"`, `OutputRaw="raw"`, `OutputJSON="json"`. Define the `Manifest` struct with ALL §12.1 fields in this order, each exported CamelCase with a `toml:"<snake_key>"` tag and a godoc comment citing §12.1: `Name`, `Detect`, `Command` (string); `Subcommand`, `BareFlags` ([]string); `PromptDelivery` (string); `PromptFlag`, `PrintFlag`, `ModelFlag`, `DefaultModel`, `SystemPromptFlag`, `ProviderFlag`, `DefaultProvider` (string); `Output` (string); `JSONField`, `RetryInstruction` (string); `StripCodeFence` (bool); `Env` (map[string]string, `toml:"env"`). Do NOT add fields beyond this list (e.g. no `experimental`) and do NOT omit any. Define `Rendered{ Args []string; StdinPayload string; DeliverViaStdin bool; Env []string }` with godoc (Args is the arg slice AFTER the command; Env is sorted "KEY=VALUE" additions). Implement `func (m Manifest) Render(model, provider, sys, user string) (*Rendered, error)` per §12.2 byte-exactly: compute `effUser := user` and, if `m.SystemPromptFlag == "" && sys != ""`, set `effUser = sys + "\n\n" + user` (prepend fallback for ALL delivery modes); build `args` by defensive-copying `m.Subcommand` into a fresh slice, then append in FIXED order `(ProviderFlag,provider)` [only if ProviderFlag!="" && provider!=""], `(ModelFlag,model)` [only if ModelFlag!="" && model!=""], `(SystemPromptFlag,sys)` [only if SystemPromptFlag!="" && sys!=""], then `m.BareFlags...` VERBATIM (keep empty-string elements like claude's `--tools ""`), then `PrintFlag` if non-empty (AFTER bare_flags — the §12.2 algorithm, NOT the §B.2/§B.6 illustration that puts -p first). Switch on `m.PromptDelivery`: `""`/`"stdin"` → StdinPayload=effUser, DeliverViaStdin=true; `"positional"` → append effUser to Args, DeliverViaStdin=false, StdinPayload=""; `"flag"` → append (PromptFlag, effUser) to Args, DeliverViaStdin=false; default → `return nil, fmt.Errorf("provider %q: unknown prompt_delivery %q (want stdin|positional|flag)", m.Name, m.PromptDelivery)`. Convert m.Env to a sorted (by key) `[]string` of `key+"="+value` and set r.Env. Return `&Rendered{Args, StdinPayload, DeliverViaStdin, Env}`. Render must NOT import os/exec, call os.Environ, resolve DefaultModel/DefaultProvider, validate Command, build an exec.Cmd, or read Output/JSONField/StripCodeFence/RetryInstruction. Doc-comment Render citing §12.2 and the prepend note.

Create `internal/provider/manifest_test.go` as `package provider` (white-box, like internal/ui/*_test.go) using the stdlib `testing` package ONLY — do NOT modify go.mod/go.sum. Tests: (1) `TestRender_GoldenProviders` — a table over the SIX providers built from external_deps.md §B.1-B.6 manifests, using sys="SYS" and user="BODY" (so prepend="SYS

BODY"); for each assert byte-exact `r.Args` (WITHOUT the command token), `r.StdinPayload`, and `r.DeliverViaStdin` using reflect.DeepEqual or element-by-element comparison: pi(model="glm-5-turbo",provider="zai")→Args `[--provider zai --model glm-5-turbo --system-prompt SYS --no-tools --no-extensions --no-skills --no-prompt-templates --no-context-files --no-session -p]`, StdinPayload="BODY", via=true; claude(model="sonnet",provider="")→Args `[--model sonnet --system-prompt SYS --setting-sources "" --tools "" --disable-slash-commands --no-chrome --no-session-persistence -p]` (print LAST per algorithm), StdinPayload="BODY", via=true; gemini(model="gemini-2.5-pro")→Args `[-m gemini-2.5-pro --approval-mode default "SYS

BODY"]`, StdinPayload="", via=false; opencode(model="anthropic/claude-sonnet-4")→Args `[run -m anthropic/claude-sonnet-4 "SYS

BODY"]`, via=false; codex(model="gpt-5", CORRECTED stdin)→Args `[exec -m gpt-5 --sandbox read-only --ask-for-approval never --ephemeral]`, StdinPayload="SYS

BODY", via=true; cursor(model="gpt-5")→Args `[--model gpt-5 --mode ask --trust -p "SYS

BODY"]` (model before bare, print last per algorithm), via=false. (2) `TestRender_NoModel` — pi with model="" omits the `--model` token. (3) `TestRender_NoProvider` — pi with provider="" omits the `--provider` token. (4) `TestRender_FlagDelivery` — synthetic PromptDelivery="flag" manifest → Args end with [PromptFlag, effUser], via=false, StdinPayload="". (5) `TestRender_EnvSorted` — Env={"B":"2","A":"1","C":"3"} → r.Env==["A=1","B=2","C=3"]. (6) `TestRender_UnknownDeliveryErrors` — PromptDelivery="telepathy" → err!=nil AND returned *Rendered==nil. (7) `TestRender_EmptySysNoPrepend` — SystemPromptFlag="" + sys="" + stdin → StdinPayload==user (no stray "

"). (8) `TestRender_DoesNotMutateManifest` — call Render twice; assert m.Subcommand/BareFlags/Env unchanged. Use `tc:=tc` + `t.Run` for the table.

Run the validation gates IN ORDER and fix any failure before declaring done: `go build ./internal/provider/` (must compile), `go vet ./internal/provider/` (clean), `test -z "$(gofmt -l internal/provider/)"` (no unformatted files), `go test ./internal/provider/` (PASS — golden table + all edge cases), `go test ./...` (whole-module integrity; Makefile `test` target green; internal/ui tests still pass).

DOCS impact: Mode A — confirm every `Manifest` field has a godoc comment citing PRD §12.1 and the `Render` method doc cites §12.2 plus the system-prompt-via-payload fallback. Create NO README.md/docs/providers/*.toml here. Confirm scope boundaries: only `internal/provider/manifest.go` and `internal/provider/manifest_test.go` were created (new package); main.go, Makefile, go.mod, go.sum, and internal/ui are unchanged; no non-stdlib imports were added; no `go mod tidy` was run; the `// Package provider` doc comment appears exactly once (in manifest.go).

successCriteria.description: `internal/provider/manifest.go` exists as the first file of a NEW `package provider` with a file-level `// Package provider` doc comment citing PRD §12, and imports ONLY stdlib (`fmt`/`sort`/`strings`); no `os/exec`, cobra, pflag, or go-toml import.

The `Manifest` struct defines ALL and ONLY the §12.1 fields (Name, Detect, Command, Subcommand, PromptDelivery, PromptFlag, PrintFlag, ModelFlag, DefaultModel, SystemPromptFlag, ProviderFlag, DefaultProvider, BareFlags, Output, JSONField, StripCodeFence, RetryInstruction, Env) with exported CamelCase names, `toml:"<snake_key>"` tags matching §12.1, and per-field godoc citing §12.1; `Rendered{Args, StdinPayload, DeliverViaStdin, Env}` and the Delivery*/Output* string constants exist.

`Render(model, provider, sys, user string) (*Rendered, error)` builds Args in the fixed §12.2 order (Subcommand, then ProviderFlag/provider, ModelFlag/model, SystemPromptFlag/sys, BareFlags, PrintFlag) — PrintFlag AFTER BareFlags (the algorithm, not the §B.2/§B.6 illustration) — guards each flag pair with non-empty checks, appends BareFlags verbatim (incl. empty strings), and applies the system-prompt prepend (`sys+"\n\n"+user`) when `SystemPromptFlag=="" && sys!="" for all three delivery modes.

The delivery switch is correct: `""`/`stdin`→StdinPayload=effUser, DeliverViaStdin=true; `positional`→append effUser, via=false; `flag`→append (PromptFlag,effUser), via=false; unknown value→non-nil error with nil *Rendered. Rendered.Args EXCLUDES the command; Rendered.Env is sorted "KEY=VALUE"; Render does not resolve defaults or build an exec.Cmd.

`internal/provider/manifest_test.go` (package provider, stdlib testing) has a golden table asserting byte-exact Args (algorithm-derived order) + StdinPayload + DeliverViaStdin for pi/claude/gemini/opencode/codex/cursor from external_deps.md §B.1-B.6 (codex PromptDelivery="stdin" with --ephemeral).

Edge-case tests pass: no-model omits --model, no-provider omits --provider, flag-delivery appends (PromptFlag,effUser), Env maps render sorted as "K=V", unknown delivery errors, empty-sys produces no stray "\n\n", and Render does not mutate the source Manifest slices.
`go test ./internal/provider/` exits 0 (PASS) and `go test ./...` exits 0 (whole-module integrity; Makefile `test` target green; internal/ui tests still pass).
`go build ./internal/provider/` and `go vet ./internal/provider/` are clean, and `gofmt -l internal/provider/` produces no output.

Scope boundaries respected: only internal/provider/manifest.go and internal/provider/manifest_test.go created; main.go, Makefile, go.mod, go.sum, and internal/ui unchanged; no README/docs/providers TOML created; no `go mod tidy`; the `// Package provider` doc comment appears exactly once.

PRD.md §12.2 (lines 493-518) — THE render algorithm (arg order + delivery switch) and the system-prompt-via-stdin/no-flag-prepend note. file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/PRD.md

PRD.md §19 (lines 1200-1280) — security: No sh -c; commands built as []string; diff delivered via stdin never interpolated into an argument (Render emits []string + stdin, never a shell string). file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/PRD.md

go-toml/v2 struct tags — binds TOML keys via `toml:"..."` tags (no auto CamelCase→snake_case); tags are inert reflect strings so manifest.go needs no go-toml import. https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2
fmt.Errorf — the unknown-delivery error constructor. https://pkg.go.dev/fmt#Errorf
Go slices/append semantics — defensive copy of Subcommand/BareFlags so Render never mutates caller slices. https://go.dev/blog/slices-intro
